name: Hanmi Bank
description_short: Hanmi Bank is a 40+ year old FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business checking, savings, SBA lending, and digital banking solutions with branch locations nationwide.
description_long:
Hanmi Bank was founded in 1982 and holds the distinction of being the first Korean American bank in the United States. The bank has grown into a significant financial institution with $9.62 billion in total assets and $5.13 billion in deposits as of September 30, 2025. It maintains a network of full-service branches and personalized loan offices across the country, serving both individual and business customers.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of services including personal checking and savings accounts, business banking with 24/7 mobile access, and specialized SBA lending programs. Hanmi Bank provides digital banking tools that allow customers to categorize spending, set budgets, visualize spending habits, and access financial planning features. For business customers, the bank offers online domestic wire transfers, relationship-based banking, and personalized guidance. The institution is FDIC-insured, providing federal deposit protection.

Hanmi Bank distinguishes itself through its focus on relationship-based banking and specialized expertise in small business lending. The bank ranks in the top 2% of SBA lenders in the U.S. according to the 7a Activity Report as of September 30, 2024, and was recognized by Forbes in 2024 as one of America's Best Banks. The bank holds a 2-star rating from Bauer Financial. For business checking accounts, Hanmi offers promotions such as earning up to 200,000 points worth up to $2,000 in cash value.

Hanmi Bank is a legitimate, well-established FDIC-insured institution with strong regulatory oversight and a long operating history. However, as a full-service traditional bank, it may have higher minimum balance requirements and less competitive rates compared to online-only banks. Customers should verify current rates and fees directly, as the website does not prominently display APY rates or specific fee schedules. The bank emphasizes security features and has recently enhanced protocols such as limiting OTP delivery to phone-only for business customers. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Business checking accounts with promotional point rewards
Spending categorization and budget tracking tools
Financial visualization and spending analysis tools
Online domestic wire transfers for business checking
Personalized loan offices and relationship-based lending
Digital banking solutions and mobile app access
pros: FDIC-insured with $9.62 billion in total assets, providing strong institutional stability
Ranked in top 2% of SBA lenders in the U.S., with specialized small business lending expertise
40+ years of operating history as the first Korean American bank in the U.S.
Recognized by Forbes in 2024 as one of America's Best Banks
Offers integrated digital financial tools including spending categorization, budget tracking, and visualization
24/7 mobile banking access with relationship-based personalized service for business customers
Network of full-service branches and loan offices nationwide for in-person banking
cons: Website does not clearly display APY rates or fee structures for comparison shopping
Specific minimum balance requirements for accounts not listed on homepage
Limited information about personal loan terms, credit card offerings, or mortgage products on main website
No mention of competitive advantages over online-only banks regarding rates or fees
Business OTP security change (email disabled) may create friction for some users accustomed to email delivery

contact.address: 3660 Wilshire Blvd Ste Ph-A, Los Angeles, CA 90010

best_for: Small business owners seeking specialized SBA lending and relationship-based banking support
Customers who value in-person branch relationships alongside digital banking convenience
Individuals and businesses seeking FDIC-insured deposits with institutional stability and 40+ year track record
Korean American communities and those seeking culturally-aware banking services
diagnosis: Hanmi Bank is best suited for small business owners seeking specialized SBA lending expertise and relationship-based service, as well as individuals who value institutional stability, branch access, and integrated digital tools. The main caveat is that customers should directly verify current rates, fees, and minimum balances, as these are not prominently displayed on the website, and compare offerings against online-only competitors before opening an account.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 3660 Wilshire Blvd Ste Ph-A, Los Angeles, CA 90010
